ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the network traffic data was recorded */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the network traffic data was recorded */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time when the network traffic data was recorded */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address of the network traffic */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address of the network traffic */,
  "bandwidth_used" REAL /* Bandwidth used during the recorded period */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Packet loss rate */,
  "latency" REAL /* Network latency */,
  "threat_level" INTEGER /* Potential security threat level */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID linked to the traffic data */,
  "gre" REAL /* Percentage of GRE traffic */,
  "icmp" REAL /* Percentage of ICMP traffic */,
  "tcp" REAL /* Percentage of TCP traffic */,
  "udp" REAL /* Percentage of UDP traffic */,
  "esp" REAL /* Percentage of ESP traffic */,
  "encap" REAL /* Percentage of ENCAP traffic */,
  "compaq_peer" INTEGER /* Percentage of Compaq-Peer traffic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date", "time")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the network protocol */,
  "protocol_type" TEXT /* Type of the protocol (e.g., transport, application) */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the protocol supports encryption */,
  "default_port" INTEGER /* Default port number used by the protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network protocol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_distribution" (
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each traffic distribution record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the traffic distribution was recorded */,
  "time_slot" TEXT /* Time slot for the distribution data (e.g., hourly, daily) */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network protocol */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of network traffic for the protocol */,
  "data_volume" REAL /* Volume of data for the specific protocol during the time slot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_distribution_date FOREIGN KEY ("date") REFERENCES network_traffic ("date"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_distribution_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES network_protocols ("protocol_id")
)
